title: Loxanthes marginata
intro: Loxanthes marginata, also known as the "Andean lavender", is a flowering plant species native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation due to its sedative effects. The bioactive constituents of Loxanthes marginata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its anxiolytic and antioxidant properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate stress and promote a restful night's sleep.
taxonomy: Loxanthes marginata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of South America. This plant is a type of gentian, characterized by its unique features and growth habits. Within the Gentianaceae family, Loxanthes marginata is further classified as part of the genus Loxanthes. The classification of Loxanthes marginata reflects its distinct characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
